questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Polonium $\left(\mathrm{P}_0^{210}\right)$ emits ${ }_2 \alpha^4$ particles and is converted into lead $\left({ }_{82} \mathrm{~Pb}^{206}\right)$. This reaction is used for producing electric power in a space mission: $\mathrm{P}_0{ }^{210}$ has half life of 138.6 days. (Given masses of the nuclei) $\left.\mathrm{P}_0^{210}=209.98264 \mathrm{amu}, \mathrm{Pb}^{206}=205.97440,,^4=4.00260 \mathrm{amu}\right)$Question: What is the initial amount of $\mathrm{P}_0^{210}$ ?
questions.solution:
${ }_{84} \mathrm{P}_0^{210} \rightarrow_{82} \mathrm{~Pb}^{206}+{ }_2 \mathrm{He}^4$
Mass converted to energy per reaction is
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta \mathrm{m}=\mathrm{m}\left({ }_{84} \mathrm{P}_0^{210}\right)-\left[\mathrm{m}\left({ }_{82} \mathrm{~Pb}^{206}\right)+\mathrm{m}\left({ }_2 \mathrm{He}^4\right)\right] \\ & =5.25 \mathrm{MeV}=8.4 \times 10^{-13} \text { Joule }\end{aligned}$
Total amount of energy required $=1.2 \times 10^7$ Joule Input Energy $=1.2 \times 10^7 \times 10=1.2 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~J}$
Number of reaction required per day
$\mathrm{n}=\frac{\mathrm{dN}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{1}{7} \times 10^{21}$ per day
If N is the number of polonium atoms required the
$-\frac{\mathrm{dN}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\lambda \mathrm{N}=\mathrm{n}=\lambda \mathrm{N}$
$\mathrm{N}=\frac{\mathrm{n}}{\lambda}=\frac{\mathrm{nT}}{0.693}=\frac{200}{7} \times 10^{21}$
Mass of each $\mathrm{P}_0-$ atom $=210 \mathrm{amu}$
Mass of $\mathrm{P}_0$ required after 693 days $=\left(\frac{200}{7}\right) \times 10^{21} \times 210 \mathrm{amu}=1.0 \mathrm{gm}$
Initial mass $\left(m_0\right)$ of $\mathrm{P}_0\left(\frac{\mathrm{~m}}{\mathrm{~m}_0}\right)=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{\mathrm{t} / \mathrm{T}}$
$\frac{\mathrm{t}}{\mathrm{T}}=\frac{693}{138.6}=5 \Rightarrow \mathrm{~m}_0=(2)^5 \times 1 \mathrm{~g}=32 \mathrm{gm}$
Initial number of $\mathrm{P}_0$ atoms
$=\mathrm{N}_0=(2)^{\imath / \mathrm{T}} \cdot \mathrm{~N}=\frac{64}{7} \times 10^{23}$

Initial activity of $\mathrm{P}_0$ :
$\mathrm{R}=-\frac{\mathrm{dN}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\lambda \mathrm{N}_0=\frac{0.693}{\mathrm{~T}} \mathrm{~N}_0=4.57 \times 10^{21}$ per day
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:A nucleus kept at rest in the free space, break up into two smaller nuclei of masses m and 2 m . Total energy generated in this fission is E . The bigger part is radioactive, emits five gamma ray photons in the direction of its velocity and finally comes to rest (given $\mathrm{h}=6.6 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{~J}-\mathrm{s}, \mathrm{m}=1.00 \times 10^{-26} \mathrm{~kg}, \mathrm{E}=3.63 \times 10^{-8} \mathrm{mc}^2, \mathrm{c}=3 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$Question:Fractional loss of mass in the fission is
questions.solution:
Total energy released will be in released in form K. Energy.
Let $V$ be the velocity of smaller nucleus.
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{1}{2} m v^2+\frac{1}{2}(2 m) \frac{v^2}{4}=E \\
& \frac{3}{4} m v^2=E
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} v & =\sqrt{\frac{4 E}{3 m}}=\sqrt{\frac{4 \times 3.63 \times 10^{-8} \times m c^2}{3 m}} \\ & =\sqrt{4 \times 1.21 \times 10^{-8} \times 9 \times 10^{-16}} \\ & =6 \times 1.1 \times 10^4 \Rightarrow 6.6 \times 10^4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\ 2 m & \times \frac{v}{2}=5 \frac{h}{\lambda}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Polonium $\left(\mathrm{P}_0^{210}\right)$ emits ${ }_2 \alpha^4$ particles and is converted into lead $\left({ }_{82} \mathrm{~Pb}^{206}\right)$. This reaction is used for producing electric power in a space mission: $\mathrm{P}_0{ }^{210}$ has half life of 138.6 days. (Given masses of the nuclei) $\left.\mathrm{P}_0^{210}=209.98264 \mathrm{amu}, \mathrm{Pb}^{206}=205.97440,,^4=4.00260 \mathrm{amu}\right)$Question: Find the initial activity of the material.
questions.solution:
${ }_{84} \mathrm{P}_0^{210} \rightarrow_{82} \mathrm{~Pb}^{206}+{ }_2 \mathrm{He}^4$
Mass converted to energy per reaction is
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta \mathrm{m}=\mathrm{m}\left({ }_{84} \mathrm{P}_0^{210}\right)-\left[\mathrm{m}\left({ }_{82} \mathrm{~Pb}^{206}\right)+\mathrm{m}\left({ }_2 \mathrm{He}^4\right)\right] \\ & =5.25 \mathrm{MeV}=8.4 \times 10^{-13} \text { Joule }\end{aligned}$
Total amount of energy required $=1.2 \times 10^7$ Joule Input Energy $=1.2 \times 10^7 \times 10=1.2 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~J}$
Number of reaction required per day
$\mathrm{n}=\frac{\mathrm{dN}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{1}{7} \times 10^{21}$ per day
If N is the number of polonium atoms required the
$-\frac{\mathrm{dN}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\lambda \mathrm{N}=\mathrm{n}=\lambda \mathrm{N}$
$\mathrm{N}=\frac{\mathrm{n}}{\lambda}=\frac{\mathrm{nT}}{0.693}=\frac{200}{7} \times 10^{21}$
Mass of each $\mathrm{P}_0-$ atom $=210 \mathrm{amu}$
Mass of $\mathrm{P}_0$ required after 693 days $=\left(\frac{200}{7}\right) \times 10^{21} \times 210 \mathrm{amu}=1.0 \mathrm{gm}$
Initial mass $\left(m_0\right)$ of $\mathrm{P}_0\left(\frac{\mathrm{~m}}{\mathrm{~m}_0}\right)=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{\mathrm{t} / \mathrm{T}}$
$\frac{\mathrm{t}}{\mathrm{T}}=\frac{693}{138.6}=5 \Rightarrow \mathrm{~m}_0=(2)^5 \times 1 \mathrm{~g}=32 \mathrm{gm}$
Initial number of $\mathrm{P}_0$ atoms
$=\mathrm{N}_0=(2)^{\imath / \mathrm{T}} \cdot \mathrm{~N}=\frac{64}{7} \times 10^{23}$

Initial activity of $\mathrm{P}_0$ :
$\mathrm{R}=-\frac{\mathrm{dN}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\lambda \mathrm{N}_0=\frac{0.693}{\mathrm{~T}} \mathrm{~N}_0=4.57 \times 10^{21}$ per day

& \frac{1}{2} m v^2+\frac{1}{2}(2 m) \frac{v^2}{4}=E \\
& \frac{3}{4} m v^2=E

$\begin{aligned} v & =\sqrt{\frac{4 E}{3 m}}=\sqrt{\frac{4 \times 3.63 \times 10^{-8} \times m c^2}{3 m}} \\ & =\sqrt{4 \times 1.21 \times 10^{-8} \times 9 \times 10^{-16}} \\ & =6 \times 1.1 \times 10^4 \Rightarrow 6.6 \times 10^4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\ 2 m & \times \frac{v}{2}=5 \frac{h}{\lambda}\end{aligned}$

questions.q: Paragraph: Few atomic masses are given ${ }_{92}^{238} \mathrm{U}=238.05079 \mathrm{u},{ }_2^4 \mathrm{He}=4.00260 \mathrm{u},{ }_{90}^{234} \mathrm{Th}=234.04363 \mathrm{u}$ ${ }_1^1 \mathrm{H}=1.007834,{ }_{91}^{237} \mathrm{~Pa}=237.065121 \mathrm{u}$. Answer the following questions on the basis of above data.Question: Calculate the energy released during the $\alpha$-decay of ${ }_{92}^{238} \mathrm{U}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Few atomic masses are given ${ }_{92}^{238} \mathrm{U}=238.05079 \mathrm{u},{ }_2^4 \mathrm{He}=4.00260 \mathrm{u},{ }_{90}^{234} \mathrm{Th}=234.04363 \mathrm{u}$ ${ }_1^1 \mathrm{H}=1.007834,{ }_{91}^{237} \mathrm{~Pa}=237.065121 \mathrm{u}$. Answer the following questions on the basis of above data.Question: Spontaneous emission of a proton from ${ }_{92}^{238} \mathrm{U}$ is
questions.solution:
If it emits proton spontaneously, the equation is not balanced in terms of atoms \& mass number.
${ }_{92}^{238} \mathrm{U} \rightarrow{ }_{91}^{237} \mathrm{~Pa}+{ }_1^4 \mathrm{H}$
$\Delta \mathrm{m}=(238.05079-237.065121-1.007834) \mathrm{u}=-0.022165 \mathrm{u}$
$\because \Delta \mathrm{m}$ is negative, so reaction is not spontaneous.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: The Rutherford - Royds experiment : The following experiment dates from 1909. The apparatus is shown below. The thin - walled glass flask, containing radon (an $\alpha$ emitter), is placed inside the thick-walled glass apparatus. The apparatus is evacuated, and the valve to the vacuum pump is then closed. After a week, additional mercury is added to the apparatus until the liquid level reaches the bottom of the tube. A high-voltage discharge is then passed through the tube, and the light emitted is observed with a spectrometer. The flask emits $\alpha$-particles when Radon decays according to equation ${ }_{86}^{222} \mathrm{Rn} \longrightarrow{ }_{84}^{218} \mathrm{Po}+{ }_2^4 \mathrm{He}$
with a half life of 3.5 days. The flask contained 222 mg of radioactive Radon. We can assume that half of the $\alpha$-particles emitted accumulate in the empty space above mercury. The volume of tube is 1 litre. Take density of mercury as $13.6 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{cc}$. Assume that $\alpha$-particles can combine with 1 stray electron or 2 stay electrons to form Helium gas, or singly ionized Helium in ground state. (Take : Atmospheric pressure $10^5 \mathrm{~Pa}$, temperature $\left.=27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{R}=25 / 3 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{mol}-\mathrm{K}\right)$Question: How many moles of Helium gas is present in the capillary tube?
questions.solution: $n_{R n}=\frac{222}{222}=1$ mole $; N_t=\frac{N_0}{2^{\frac{t}{t_{1 / 2}}}}=\frac{N_0}{2^{7 / 3.5}}=\frac{N_0}{4} \quad \therefore$ Number of nuclei decayed $=\frac{3 \mathrm{~N}_0}{4}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Paragraph: The Rutherford - Royds experiment : The following experiment dates from 1909. The apparatus is shown below. The thin - walled glass flask, containing radon (an $\alpha$ emitter), is placed inside the thick-walled glass apparatus. The apparatus is evacuated, and the valve to the vacuum pump is then closed. After a week, additional mercury is added to the apparatus until the liquid level reaches the bottom of the tube. A high-voltage discharge is then passed through the tube, and the light emitted is observed with a spectrometer. The flask emits $\alpha$-particles when Radon decays according to equation ${ }_{86}^{222} \mathrm{Rn} \longrightarrow{ }_{84}^{218} \mathrm{Po}+{ }_2^4 \mathrm{He}$
with a half life of 3.5 days. The flask contained 222 mg of radioactive Radon. We can assume that half of the $\alpha$-particles emitted accumulate in the empty space above mercury. The volume of tube is 1 litre. Take density of mercury as $13.6 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{cc}$. Assume that $\alpha$-particles can combine with 1 stray electron or 2 stay electrons to form Helium gas, or singly ionized Helium in ground state. (Take : Atmospheric pressure $10^5 \mathrm{~Pa}$, temperature $\left.=27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{R}=25 / 3 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{mol}-\mathrm{K}\right)$Question: What is the pressure at point A ?
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
